== Notes for [[Item:Q90969|Q90969]] ==

Caecina Paetus From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia Caecina Paetus was condemned to death on a charge of disloyalty by the emperor Claudius in 42 AD. He chose to commit suicide rather than face the emperor's wrath. His wife Arria stabbed herself first in order to give him the courage to do this and handed him the dagger saying "Non dolet, Paete!" ("It doesn't hurt, Paetus"). Contents [hide] 1 References 1.1 Primary 1.2 Secondary 2 External links References[edit]

Primary[edit] Pliny, Letters III.xvi.6 Secondary[edit] Georges Duby, Michelle Perrot, Pauline Schmitt. A History of Women in the West, Volume I. Belknap Press.2000 External links[edit]

Women's life in Greece and Rome Categories: 1st-century RomansSuicides by sharp instrument in Italy

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caecina_Paetus

[[Category:Possibly derived from wikipedia]]
[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
